title: Volume of a rectangular prism
problem: A rectangular prism has length $4 \text{ cm}$, width $6 \text{ cm}$ and height $5 \text{ cm}$. Find its volume.

steps.instruction: Write the volume formula for a rectangular prism.

title: Volume of a triangular prism
problem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with base $8 \text{ cm}$ and perpendicular height $3 \text{ cm}$. The prism is $7 \text{ cm}$ long. Find its volume.

steps.instruction: Find the area of the triangular cross-section.
Multiply the cross-sectional area by the length of the prism.

title: Volume of a tank and capacity
problem: A rectangular water tank is $90 \text{ cm}$ long, $60 \text{ cm}$ wide and $50 \text{ cm}$ deep. Water fills $\tfrac{3}{4}$ of its capacity. How many litres of water are in the tank? ($1 \text{ litre} = 1000 \text{ cm}^3$)

steps.instruction: Find the full volume of the tank using $V = lwh$.
Find the volume of water, which is $\tfrac{3}{4}$ of the full volume.
Convert from $\text{cm}^3$ to litres by dividing by $1000$.
